title: tragacantha lanceolata
intro: Tragacantha lanceolata, also known as Needle Bush, is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its sharp, needle-like leaves and small white flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Tragacantha lanceolata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat a range of health issues.
